abstract.text: The
enum defines roles based on
and
A keyboard key that deletes content.
A menu container.
A popup button control that reveals additional options when activated.
A checkbox control that can be checked or unchecked independently.
An alert that provides important, time-sensitive information to the user.
A segmented control container that groups segment buttons.
A grid container that arranges items in rows and columns.
A table container that displays data in rows and columns.
An individual column within a table.
A stepper control for incrementing or decrementing a value.
A generic grouping container.
A window top-level container.
A web content view that displays web-based content.
A back button that navigates back.
A text field control that accepts single-line user text entry.
A tab control within a tab bar for switching between different views or sections.
A tab group container for grouping tabbed content.
A layout item container for individual layout elements.
An individual item within a menu bar.
A navigation bar container that provides navigation context.
A Touch Bar container (macOS Touch Bar).
Returns the name of this role.
A scroll view container for scrollable content.
A collection view container that displays items in a grid or custom layout.
A radio group container for grouping radio buttons.
A toggle control that can be switched on or off.
A combo box control that combines text input with a dropdown selection.
An adjustable element that allows continuous value changes through a range of values.
A toolbar button control within a toolbar.
A cell view inside a collection view or table view.
An application top-level container.
A level indicator that displays a value within a range.
A popover overlay that presents content relative to an anchor.
A slider control for selecting a value from a continuous range.
An individual item within a status bar.
A splitter control between split group panes.
A disclosure triangle control that reveals or hides additional content.
An image element that displays visual content.
A decrement arrow control within an adjustable element.
A date picker control for selecting dates and times.
A toolbar container that provides contextual actions.
Static text content that displays information and can’t change.
A switch control that can be toggled on or off.
A drawer container that slides in from the edge.
A picker wheel control for scrolling through values.
A color well control for selecting colors.
A tab bar container that groups tab controls.
A picker control for selecting values from a list.
A secure text field control that hides input for privacy.
A map element that provides geographic or spatial information.
A header that divides content into sections, such as the title of a navigation bar.
A handle control for dragging and resizing.
A value indicator that displays a numeric value within a context.
An icon element that displays a small visual symbol.
A progress indicator that shows completion of a task.
A dock item container (macOS dock items).
A ruler container for measurement display (not used on iOS).
An individual row within a table.
A button control that can be tapped to perform actions.
A menu button control that opens a menu when activated.
A link control that navigates to other content or external resources.
An individual button segment within a segmented control.
A split group container that divides content into resizable panes.
An element that doesn’t fit into other specific categories.
A keyboard container.
A layout area container for organizing content regions.
An increment arrow control within an adjustable element.
A text view control that accepts multi-line user text entry.
An outline container that displays hierarchical data.
The
enum mirrors
An activity indicator that shows an ongoing operation.
Footer text that provides closing or supplementary information for a section.
A status bar container that displays system or app status.
A matte container that provides a background.
A toggle button that toggles between states.
A menu bar container.
The
enum mirrors
An individual item within a menu or dropdown list.
The
protocol requires the
property.
A modal dialog overlay that requires user interaction.
A rating indicator that displays a rating value.
The
enum defines roles based on
and
A help tag overlay (tooltip).
A keyboard key control (letters, numbers, special keys).
A relevance indicator that displays a relevance score.
A sheet overlay that slides in from the edge.
A search field control for entering search queries.
An individual row within an outline view.
A scroll bar control for navigating scrollable content.
A browser top-level container.
A timeline container that displays chronological content.
A page indicator control for switching between pages.
A radio button control for exclusive selection within a group.
Checks if the role is defined, e.g. any role except
A ruler marker control within a ruler (not used on iOS).
